assertion_results.text: The review comment about transaction wrapping (id 30001) is classified as a code change request and triggers sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30001.md states '## Classification: Code Change Request' (line 9) and '## Action\nSub-task created to wrap the three UPDATE statements in a database transaction.' (lines 21-22). subtask-30001.md exists in the outputs directory confirming sub-task creation. report.md confirms in the Review Comment Classifications table: '30001 | ... | Code change request | Yes'.

assertion_results.text: The review comment about adding an index (id 30002) is classified as suggestion — the reviewer uses suggestive language ('should also', 'would help') and the classification output (review-30002.md) explains this reasoning
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md states '## Classification: Suggestion' (line 9). The Reasoning section explicitly analyzes the suggestive language: '"should also add" -- while "should" can be directive in some contexts, "should also" suggests an additional improvement beyond the core requirement, not a mandatory fix' and '"would help" -- conditional language indicating a beneficial but optional enhancement' and '"Something like:" -- presents the SQL as an illustrative example rather than a required implementation' (lines 16-18). report.md confirms classification as 'Suggestion' with 'No' sub-task.

assertion_results.text: The nit comment about context message (id 30003) is classified as nit and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30003.md states '## Classification: Nit' (line 9) and '## Action\nNo sub-task created. Nits do not trigger sub-task creation.' (lines 17-19). No subtask-30003.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms: '30003 | ... | Nit | No'.

assertion_results.text: The question comment about GET behavior (id 30004) is classified as question and does NOT trigger sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: review-30004.md states '## Classification: Question' (line 9) and '## Action\nNo sub-task created. Questions do not trigger sub-task creation.' (lines 19-20). No subtask-30004.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms: '30004 | ... | Question | No'.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description follows the task-description-template.md format: contains Repository, Description, Files to Modify, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md (the only sub-task) contains all required sections: '## Repository' (line 1, value: trustify-backend), '## Description' (line 3), '## Files to Modify' (line 8), '## Implementation Notes' (line 11), '## Acceptance Criteria' (line 17). It also contains '## Test Requirements' (line 23) which is a required section per the template.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Target PR section with the PR URL https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744 (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Target PR' (line 27) followed by 'https://github.com/trustify/trustify-backend/pull/744' (line 28). This matches the required URL exactly.

assertion_results.text: Each sub-task description includes a Review Context section with the original review comment text (constraint 1.12)
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-30001.md contains '## Review Context' (line 30) with '**Comment ID:** 30001', '**Author:** reviewer-a', '**File:** modules/fundamental/src/sbom/service/sbom.rs (line 60)', and the full original comment text: '"The `soft_delete` method should run all three UPDATE statements inside a single database transaction...' (lines 31-34).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only .md files: review-30001.md, review-30002.md, review-30003.md, review-30004.md, subtask-30001.md, and report.md. A glob search for code files (*.rs, *.py, *.js, *.ts, *.toml, *.yaml, *.yml, *.json, *.sql) in the outputs directory returned no results. All output files are classification reports, sub-task descriptions, or the verification report — no source code files were created or modified.

assertion_results.text: The skill reads and processes all PR review comments before generating findings (constraint 1.10)
assertion_results.evidence: All four review comments (30001, 30002, 30003, 30004) have corresponding classification files (review-30001.md through review-30004.md). The report.md contains a 'Review Comment Classifications' table listing all four comments with their classifications. The report's summary paragraph references all four: '1 code change request (transaction wrapping), 1 suggestion (index), 1 nit (context message), 1 question (GET behavior)' (line 6).

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains no mention of merging, auto-merge, or any merge action. The overall result is 'WARN' (line 17), and the report ends with verification details. There is no indication that any merge operation was performed or initiated.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | tests/api/sbom_delete.rs is a new file; no modified or deleted test files |'. This explicitly states ADDITIVE classification with the correct justification.

assertion_results.text: Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002 (index suggestion) — the review classification output (review-30002.md) or the report's Style/Conventions analysis explains whether the suggestion matches a documented or demonstrated project convention
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md contains a detailed '### Convention Upgrade Eligibility Evaluation' section (lines 22-34) that evaluates three checks: (1) CONVENTIONS.md check — finding no documented convention requiring indexes on soft-delete columns, (2) Codebase pattern check — finding insufficient evidence of established pattern, (3) Performance-related scrutiny — distinguishing general best practice from project-specific conventions. The conclusion states: 'No convention upgrade is warranted. The suggestion does not match any documented convention in the project, and there is no established codebase pattern' (lines 33-34). report.md also summarizes: 'The index suggestion (comment 30002) was evaluated for convention upgrade but no documented convention or established codebase pattern supports mandatory index creation on soft-delete columns' (line 19).

assertion_results.text: Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) does NOT result in a sub-task — the suggestion classification is correct (suggestive language, no directive) and no project convention in the fixture data backs an upgrade from suggestion to code change request
assertion_results.evidence: review-30002.md classification is 'Suggestion' (line 9), and the Action section states 'No sub-task created. The suggestion is not backed by a documented project convention or established codebase pattern.' (line 38). No subtask-30002.md file exists in the outputs directory. report.md confirms: '30002 | ... | Suggestion | No' in the Review Comment Classifications table.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — the 3-criteria detection (author github-actions[bot], marker ## Eval Results, footer sdlc-workflow/run-evals) found no matches, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | PASS | All 5 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected (each test covers distinct behavior with different setup/assertions); Eval Quality: N/A |'. The Test Quality row explicitly states 'Eval Quality: N/A', confirming that eval quality detection found no matches and does not affect the Test Quality combination (which is PASS).
